metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Verify whether `.codex/config.toml` needs to be created (not merely updated) and, if so, specify its full required content in the plan — the file does not currently exist in the repo.
evidence: Plan: '.codex/config.toml currently falls back to CLAUDE.md with a comment saying it's temporary "until the guidance is migrated"'
Plan AC3: 'Update `.codex/config.toml` reflects the completed migration (comment updated; Codex reads AGENTS.md natively)' with proving command `rg -n 'AGENTS.md|migrated' .codex/config.toml`
read_file('.codex/config.toml') → ENOENT
list_directory('.') shows no `.codex/` entry
search_files('temporary.*until.*migrated') → no matches anywhere in repo
Prior plan-review cache hit: `.rebar/cache/plan-review/5b01-add0-e970-44bd/8ea74c74e0495768a40e.json` also flagged this exact absence
findings.finding: `.codex/config.toml` does not exist in the repository. The plan treats it as a primary in-scope edit target, asserts it contains `project_doc_fallback_filenames=["CLAUDE.md"]`, and quotes a 'temporary…until the guidance is migrated' comment — all unverifiable because the file is absent. A separate search for 'temporary.*until.*migrated' returns zero repo hits, and no `.codex/` directory is present in the root listing.
findings.location: Why / Scope / AC3 (`.codex/config.toml` edit target and proving command)
scenarios: Implementer opens `.codex/config.toml` to update the comment and finds the file does not exist — must decide whether to create it, skip it, or block on clarification.
The AC3 proving command (`rg -n 'AGENTS.md|migrated' .codex/config.toml`) will error on a missing file, causing the CI gate to fail or produce misleading output.
If the file genuinely needs to be created (rather than updated), the content, format, and Codex schema are entirely unspecified in the plan.
findings.suggested_fix: Either (a) confirm the file is created by a dependency story (S2/S3) and add an explicit ordering guard, or (b) add a 'create with content X' step to this story's What/Scope and update AC3's proving command accordingly.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Confirm that `.agents/rules/rebar.md` (and its parent directory) is created by a dependency story (S2 or S3) and add an explicit 'this file must exist before this story can run' guard, or specify that this story creates it.
evidence: Plan What: 'reconcile `.agents/rules/rebar.md` with `AGENTS.md` (a pointer or aligned), with no drift'
Plan AC4 proving: 'reviewer confirms `.agents/rules/rebar.md` matches AGENTS.md content or points at it'
list_directory('.agents') → ENOENT (directory does not exist)
No `.agents/` entry visible in root directory listing
findings.finding: `.agents/rules/rebar.md` does not exist in the repository. The plan treats it as an in-scope edit target ('reconcile `.agents/rules/rebar.md` with `AGENTS.md`') and AC4 asks a reviewer to confirm it 'matches AGENTS.md content or points at it' — but the `.agents/` directory itself is absent.
scenarios: Implementer looking for `.agents/rules/rebar.md` to reconcile finds neither the file nor the directory.
AC4's reviewer-confirmation step fails because there is no file to review.
If the file is supposed to be created by S2/S3, work in this story cannot proceed until that dependency lands — but no ordering guard is stated beyond 'Depends on S2 and S3'.
findings.suggested_fix: Add an explicit dependency-gate note: 'AC4 requires `.agents/rules/rebar.md` to exist (created by S2/S3); this story must not begin until that file is present. If S2/S3 do not create it, add a creation step here.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Confirm `.codex/config.toml` exists (or will exist after a dependency story lands); if it must be created here, add a CREATION note and verify it is in-scope.
evidence: `list_directory .codex` → ENOENT (directory absent from repo snapshot)
Prior review cache entry 8ea74c7 confirms: '`.codex/config.toml` does not exist as a repo-tracked file'
Plan text: 'In: `.codex/config.toml`, `.agents/rules/rebar.md`, the reference updates…'
findings.finding: `.codex/config.toml` does not exist as a discoverable repo-tracked file — the `.codex/` directory is absent from the repo root. The plan names it as a primary in-scope edit target (Scope section: 'In: `.codex/config.toml`') and describes its current content (`project_doc_fallback_filenames=["CLAUDE.md"]`, 'temporary…until migrated' comment). An executor following this plan would have no file to edit.
scenarios: Executor runs the plan and finds no `.codex/` directory to edit; AC3's rg command (`rg -n 'AGENTS.md|migrated' .codex/config.toml`) returns a non-zero exit code, failing the gate even if the file is created as part of execution.
If the file genuinely doesn't exist and needs to be CREATED, that is a new-file creation (CREATION = new behavior = out-of-scope concern), not an update.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Confirm `.agents/rules/rebar.md` exists (or is created by an upstream dependency story); if it is to be created here, reclassify the AC as a CREATION and verify in-scope.
evidence: `list_directory .agents` → ENOENT (directory absent from repo snapshot)
Plan text: 'In: `.codex/config.toml`, `.agents/rules/rebar.md`, the reference updates…'
AC4: '`.agents/rules/rebar.md` is reconciled with `AGENTS.md` (a pointer or aligned), with no drift.'
findings.finding: `.agents/rules/rebar.md` does not exist as a discoverable repo-tracked file — the `.agents/` directory is absent from the repo root. The plan names it as a primary in-scope edit target and dedicates AC4 to it ('`.agents/rules/rebar.md` is reconciled with `AGENTS.md`').
scenarios: Executor cannot reconcile a file that doesn't exist; AC4 proving step ('reviewer confirms `.agents/rules/rebar.md` matches AGENTS.md content or points at it') vacuously passes or errors.
If this file must be created here it is a CREATION, not a reconciliation — scope classification changes.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify the `.codex/config.toml` edit target: if the intent is to update the user-side Codex shim template, rename the target to `scripts/session-shims/codex-config.toml` (the actual repo artifact) and update AC#3's proving command accordingly; if a repo-side `.codex/config.toml` is genuinely needed, add a note that it must be created and explain what `project_doc_fallback_filenames` does in that context.
evidence: `.codex/` directory absent from repo listing
`scripts/session-shims/codex-config.toml` is the actual Codex shim — has no `project_doc_fallback_filenames` key, only `[shell_environment_policy]`
docs/session-id-shims.md:90 describes `codex-config.toml` as a user-side copy: 'Copy scripts/session-shims/codex-config.toml into ~/.codex/config.toml'
Zero codebase hits for `project_doc_fallback_filenames`
AC#3 proving command `rg -n 'AGENTS.md|migrated' .codex/config.toml` targets a non-existent repo path
findings.finding: The plan's `.codex/config.toml` edit target does not exist as a repo-tracked file: the directory `.codex/` is absent from the repo, and the actual Codex shim lives at `scripts/session-shims/codex-config.toml` (a user-side copy-paste fragment). That file contains no `project_doc_fallback_filenames` key — the key the plan describes updating. AC#3's proving command (`rg -n 'AGENTS.md|migrated' .codex/config.toml`) will fail or return nothing because the file path is wrong. The mechanism for this deliverable is therefore broken as specified.
findings.location: Design decisions section ('Default: keep the fallback line but update the comment'); AC#3 ('`.codex/config.toml` reflects the completed migration')
scenarios: Implementer cannot find `.codex/config.toml` in the repo and either creates it (wrong — it's a user-side file) or skips the AC (leaving it unverified)
AC#3 proving command returns no output or an error, making the criterion unverifiable
findings.suggested_fix: Change the plan's `.codex/config.toml` references to `scripts/session-shims/codex-config.toml` and update AC#3's proving command to `rg -n 'AGENTS.md|migrated' scripts/session-shims/codex-config.toml`. If `project_doc_fallback_filenames` is a real Codex key, add a comment in that file; if it does not exist in the Codex schema, remove that part of the design decision and note only the comment-update in the existing shim.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Verify against the Codex CLI config reference (`https://developers.openai.com/codex/config-reference`) that `project_doc_fallback_filenames` is a real schema key and that Codex natively prioritizes `AGENTS.md`; if the file/key do not exist in-repo, either create `.codex/config.toml` with the correct schema or update AC3 to reflect the actual Codex shim location and capability.
evidence: Plan: 'Codex reads AGENTS.md natively as primary, so project_doc_fallback_filenames=["CLAUDE.md"] becomes moot once AGENTS.md exists'
Plan: '.codex/config.toml currently falls back to CLAUDE.md with a comment saying it\'s temporary "until the guidance is migrated"'
ABSENCE: `list_directory('.')` shows no `.codex/` directory in the repo root
ABSENCE: `search_files('project_doc_fallback_filenames')` returns zero hits outside `.rebar/cache/` prior-review artifacts — the key does not appear in any live repo file
The only Codex shim in the repo (`scripts/session-shims/codex-config.toml`) contains only `[shell_environment_policy] set = { AI_AGENT = "codex" }` — no `project_doc_fallback_filenames` key
Codex config reference URL (`https://developers.openai.com/codex/config-reference`) cited in docs but not verifiable from this environment — evidence class: Unverified
findings.finding: The plan asserts two unverified Codex CLI capabilities that are load-bearing for AC3: (a) 'Codex reads AGENTS.md natively as primary' and (b) that `.codex/config.toml` currently contains a `project_doc_fallback_filenames=["CLAUDE.md"]` key with a 'temporary…until migrated' comment. Neither assertion can be confirmed: `.codex/config.toml` does not exist anywhere in the repo (no `.codex/` directory visible), the only Codex shim is `scripts/session-shims/codex-config.toml` (which contains only `[shell_environment_policy]` — no `project_doc_fallback_filenames`), and no search hit for the key exists outside cached prior-review artifacts. The Codex config reference URL is cited but not reachable for verification — evidence class: Unverified. If `project_doc_fallback_filenames` is not a real Codex config key, or if Codex does not natively prioritize AGENTS.md, the entire rationale for the AC3 edit collapses, and the AC3 prove command (`rg -n 'AGENTS.md|migrated' .codex/config.toml`) will error on a missing file.
findings.location: Design decisions section ('Codex reads AGENTS.md natively as primary, so project_doc_fallback_filenames=["CLAUDE.md"] becomes moot'); Why section ('`.codex/config.toml` currently falls back to CLAUDE.md with a comment saying it's temporary'); AC3 and its prove command
scenarios: Implementer tries to update `.codex/config.toml` and finds the file does not exist; must decide whether to create it, skip it, or block on clarification — AC3 is unprovable as written.

The `project_doc_fallback_filenames` key may not exist in the Codex CLI config schema at all; if Codex does not support this key, the entire fallback mechanism described in the Why/Design section is a phantom.

If 'Codex reads AGENTS.md natively as primary' is incorrect or version-gated, the migration rationale (AGENTS.md existence makes the fallback moot) does not hold, and the correct shim update may differ substantially.
findings.suggested_fix: Check the Codex config-reference docs for the `project_doc_fallback_filenames` key. If it exists and `.codex/config.toml` is intended as a repo-tracked project-level config, create the file and add it to scope. If Codex's AGENTS.md prioritization is built-in and requires no config, state that explicitly and drop the 'keep the fallback line' instruction from the design decision — the AC3 prove command should then target whichever file actually exists.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Extend the AC2 proving command to include `docs/plan-review-criteria-guide.md` (and optionally `docs/adr/0043-operator-attested-completion-evidence.md`) so all criteria-text update targets are checked in a single assertion.
evidence: AC2 proving command: `[ "$(rg -c 'CLAUDE\.md' src/rebar/llm/plan_review/criteria_routing.json src/rebar/llm/reviewers/plan_review_T5e.md | awk …)" = 0 ]`
`docs/plan-review-criteria-guide.md:467: - A novel architectural decision is captured in an ADR / CLAUDE.md / design-doc update.`
Plan scope: 'docs/{…plan-review-criteria-guide…}.md' is an in-scope update target
findings.finding: The AC2 proving command checks only `src/rebar/llm/plan_review/criteria_routing.json` and `src/rebar/llm/reviewers/plan_review_T5e.md` for residual `CLAUDE.md` references, but `docs/plan-review-criteria-guide.md` line 467 contains an identical `CLAUDE.md` occurrence ('A novel architectural decision is captured in an ADR / CLAUDE.md / design-doc update') that the plan lists as an in-scope update target. After the edit, the AC2 command would pass even if the guide is missed.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria AC2 — proving command; `docs/plan-review-criteria-guide.md` line 467
scenarios: Executor updates criteria_routing.json and plan_review_T5e.md but forgets docs/plan-review-criteria-guide.md; AC2's rg command returns 0 and the gate passes, leaving a stale CLAUDE.md reference in the guide.
findings.suggested_fix: Replace the AC2 command with: `rg -c 'CLAUDE\.md' src/rebar/llm/plan_review/criteria_routing.json src/rebar/llm/reviewers/plan_review_T5e.md docs/plan-review-criteria-guide.md | awk -F: '{s+=$2} END{print s+0}'` and assert the result equals 0.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Either tag AC5 with `[operator-attested]` (since the evidence lives on a ticket, outside the repo) and specify the concrete attestation form (e.g. a ticket comment ID or a line in CHANGELOG), or restate the AC as a repo-checkable artifact (e.g. a `docs/decisions/per-tool-pointers.md` stub committed to the branch).
evidence: The decision is already made in the plan body ('Default on per-tool pointers: do NOT add…defer to a follow-up ticket') so the only remaining act is ticketing/commenting
Without the `[operator-attested]` tag, the criterion reads as an in-session obligation with no executable proof
ADR 0043 operator-attested exception requires the exact case-insensitive prefix to be present
findings.finding: AC5 ('a comment on this ticket records the decision') is not tagged `[operator-attested]` and has no in-session proving command; an executing agent cannot complete or verify this AC deterministically within a single session.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria – AC5: 'The per-tool-pointer decision is recorded (default: defer; none added here). Proving: a comment on this ticket records the decision.'
scenarios: An automated agent has no mechanism to post a comment on a ticket; it cannot satisfy this AC and cannot verify it was satisfied.

A human executor marks AC5 done by pointing to the plan body, which is already written — but that means the AC is trivially true before any work begins, making it a one-time transition not a durable end-state.
findings.suggested_fix: Prepend `[operator-attested]` to the AC5 checkbox text and change 'Proving' to: 'Proving: link to the ticket comment or CHANGELOG entry recording the defer decision.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Replace the AC3 proving command with one that asserts the OLD 'temporary…until' comment text is ABSENT and a new AGENTS.md-referencing comment is present (two separate assertions).
evidence: Defect taxonomy item 4: fixture invalidity — the command does not verify the OLD comment was removed
The plan says the comment currently reads 'temporary…until the guidance is migrated'; the word 'migrated' is already in the old comment, so the proving command already passes on the unchanged file
findings.finding: AC3's proving command (`rg -n 'AGENTS.md|migrated' .codex/config.toml`) can pass without the specific 'temporary…until migrated' comment being updated, because either token may already be present in the file before the change is applied.
scenarios: An executor skips the config.toml edit; the proving command passes because 'migrated' is in the pre-existing comment; AC3 is incorrectly marked green.
findings.suggested_fix: `rg -q 'temporary' .codex/config.toml && echo 'FAIL: old temporary comment still present' && exit 1; rg -q 'AGENTS\.md' .codex/config.toml && echo 'OK' || (echo 'FAIL: AGENTS.md not mentioned' && exit 1)`

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] State the default approach for `.agents/rules/rebar.md` — pick one: convert it to a one-line pointer to `AGENTS.md`, OR specify that its content must be mechanically reconciled with `AGENTS.md` (and name how drift will be detected ongoing).
evidence: Plan text: 'a pointer or aligned' — two meaningfully different outcomes with different maintenance implications
The plan's Design Decisions section does not resolve this fork
An executing agent must choose without guidance
findings.finding: AC4 does not specify whether `.agents/rules/rebar.md` should become a pointer to AGENTS.md or a content-aligned copy; the plan says 'a pointer or aligned' without picking a default, leaving a real design fork to the executor.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria – AC4: '.agents/rules/rebar.md is reconciled with AGENTS.md (a pointer or aligned), with no drift.'
scenarios: An agent writes a full content-aligned duplicate; a later author edits only one, creating the very drift the story aims to prevent.
An agent writes a thin pointer; the CI drift-check is undefined, so the AC passes vacuously.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a design-decision bullet: 'Default: convert `.agents/rules/rebar.md` to a one-line pointer (`See AGENTS.md`) — no maintained copy, no drift risk.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Strengthen the AC1 proving command to assert the expected count of remaining intentional hits (e.g. exactly 1 — the thin pointer file itself), using a shell assertion that fails on unexpected matches.
evidence: Defect taxonomy item 5: missing cardinality assertion when the criterion names a count
The AC says 'no un-updated reference remains' but the command does not assert zero un-intentional hits
The only guard is a human reading the output
findings.finding: AC1's proving command does not assert a cardinality — it prints hits and relies on manual inspection; a residual stale reference would not cause the command to fail, so the AC is not machine-verifiable as written.
scenarios: An agent runs the command, sees one hit it doesn't recognise as intentional, and still marks the AC green because the command exited 0 (rg exits 0 on matches).
findings.suggested_fix: `rg -n 'CLAUDE\.md' --glob '!CLAUDE.md' --glob '!.rebar/**' --glob '!bridge_state/**' --glob '!docs/experiments/**' | grep -v 'CLAUDE.md:' && echo 'FAIL: unexpected CLAUDE.md reference' && exit 1 || echo 'OK'`  — or use `rg -c` piped through `awk` with an `-eq 0` assertion.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the exact replacement text for the `T5e.documentation` check string in `criteria_routing.json` and the parallel prose in `plan_review_T5e.md` (e.g., `"A novel architectural decision is captured in an ADR / AGENTS.md / design-doc update."` or a tool-agnostic equivalent), so the executor has a canonical target rather than guessing.
evidence: AC #2 proof command: `rg -c 'CLAUDE\.md' src/rebar/llm/plan_review/criteria_routing.json src/rebar/llm/reviewers/plan_review_T5e.md` — specifies only that the count must reach 0, not what the replacement text is.
criteria_routing.json line 407: `"check": "A novel architectural decision is captured in an ADR / CLAUDE.md / design-doc update."`
plan_review_T5e.md line 10: `…captured in an ADR / CLAUDE.md / design doc update…` — a live LLM instruction string.
The plan's 'What' section says 'update the plan-review criteria text that names CLAUDE.md' but gives no replacement wording.
findings.finding: AC #2 mandates that `criteria_routing.json` (T5e.documentation check string) and `plan_review_T5e.md` reach zero `CLAUDE.md` hits, but nowhere in the plan is the replacement wording for these live LLM decision-rule strings specified. An executor changing a runtime-consumed prompt string without a canonical replacement risks silently degrading the check's meaning (e.g., narrowing it to `AGENTS.md` only, or leaving an awkward partial sentence).
scenarios: Executor replaces `CLAUDE.md` with `AGENTS.md` alone, making the check narrowly tool-specific rather than tool-agnostic.
Executor deletes the `/ CLAUDE.md` token and leaves `ADR /  / design-doc` with a double-slash artifact that confuses the LLM reviewer.
Executor substitutes a phrase that changes the semantic intent of the T5e documentation sub-check.
findings.suggested_fix: In the plan's 'What' or AC #2 section, add the canonical replacement: change `ADR / CLAUDE.md / design-doc update` → `ADR / AGENTS.md / design-doc update` (or `ADR / canonical guidance doc / design-doc update` if tool-agnosticism is preferred). Repeat the same replacement in `plan_review_T5e.md` line 10.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a complementary verify step to AC#1 that confirms replacement references to AGENTS.md are present in the swept files (e.g., `rg -l 'AGENTS\.md' CONTRIBUTING.md README.md ...` or an equivalent positive assertion).
evidence: AC#1 proves: 'rg -n \'CLAUDE\.md\' --glob ...' — absence of old string only
'For migrations, criteria verify BOTH removal and replacement' — rubric requirement
No complementary rg for 'AGENTS.md' in the same file set
findings.finding: AC#1's verify command checks only that CLAUDE.md references are REMOVED but does not verify that those references are REPLACED with AGENTS.md pointers — the migration verification is one-sided and a file could be updated to remove the reference without adding the correct replacement.
scenarios: A developer removes a CLAUDE.md reference from CONTRIBUTING.md without adding the AGENTS.md replacement; AC#1's verify command passes but the migration is incomplete.
findings.suggested_fix: Append to AC#1: 'Also proving: `rg -n 'AGENTS\.md' CONTRIBUTING.md README.md CHANGELOG.md rebar.toml docs/...` returns hits in each file that previously referenced CLAUDE.md.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify AC#4 to specify a single preferred approach for `.agents/rules/rebar.md` (either a pointer to AGENTS.md or aligned content with a stated sync mechanism) rather than leaving both as equivalent options.
evidence: AC#4 text: 'a pointer or aligned, with no drift'
Verify step: 'matches AGENTS.md content or points at it' — two structurally different outcomes
The 'Why' section says the file 'must not drift' implying full alignment, but 'or points at it' allows a one-line pointer instead
findings.finding: AC#4's verify step permits two fundamentally different implementations ('matches AGENTS.md content' vs 'points at it') without specifying which outcome is preferred, making the criterion untestable and creating implementer ambiguity.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC#4: 'Proving: reviewer confirms .agents/rules/rebar.md matches AGENTS.md content or points at it.'
scenarios: One implementer creates a pointer (one line), another syncs full content — both pass AC#4, but the 'no drift' guarantee works very differently in each case.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Record the per-tool-pointer deferral decision and the 'pointer-not-duplicate' constraint in a repo-visible location (e.g., a brief section in AGENTS.md, a design doc update, or a new/updated ADR) rather than solely in a ticket comment.
evidence: Plan states: 'if any are added later they must be pointers to AGENTS.md, not duplicated content'
AC: 'Proving: a comment on this ticket records the decision' — ticket comments are not repo-discoverable
The constraint 'must be pointers, not duplicated content' is an evolving rule that needs to be findable from the repo
findings.finding: The per-tool-pointer deferral decision (do NOT add .github/copilot-instructions.md, .cursor/rules, or a Gemini config) is recorded only 'in a comment on this ticket' — not in any versioned repo file. Future contributors and AI tools won't discover this decision, risking drift (e.g., someone later adds a .cursor/rules with duplicated content rather than a pointer).
findings.location: Design decisions section / AC bullet 5: 'a comment on this ticket records the decision'
scenarios: A contributor adds a .cursor/rules file months later without knowing the pointer-not-duplicate rule
A new AI tool integration is set up by someone who never sees the original ticket comment
findings.suggested_fix: Add a short section to AGENTS.md (or an ADR stub) noting: 'Per-tool pointer files (.github/copilot-instructions.md, .cursor/rules, etc.) are deferred. When added, they MUST be thin pointers to AGENTS.md — no duplicated content.' This makes the constraint discoverable via `rg` or normal repo browsing.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a citation (spike ticket ID, Codex docs URL, or S0/S2/S3 ticket reference) that grounds the claim 'Codex reads AGENTS.md natively as primary' so an implementer can verify the capability before executing the `.codex/config.toml` edit.
evidence: Plan text: 'Codex reads AGENTS.md natively as primary, so project_doc_fallback_filenames=["CLAUDE.md"] becomes moot once AGENTS.md exists.'
Plan text: '(spike-validated)' — no spike ticket ID or documentation link is provided
.codex/ directory does not exist in the repo (confirmed via list_directory)
Cache entry .rebar/cache/plan-review/5b01-add0-e970-44bd/8ea74c74e0495768a40e.json independently flags: '`.codex/config.toml` does not exist as a repo-tracked file'
findings.finding: The plan asserts 'Codex reads AGENTS.md natively as primary' as a spike-validated resolved fact, but provides no traceable evidence — no spike ticket ID, no Codex documentation citation, and no link to the S0/S2/S3 work that supposedly validated this. This is a load-bearing capability assertion: AC3 and the entire `.codex/config.toml` edit strategy hinge on it. Additionally, `.codex/config.toml` does not exist as a repo-tracked file, making its described current content unverifiable from the plan.
findings.location: Design decisions section: 'Codex reads AGENTS.md natively as primary, so project_doc_fallback_filenames=["CLAUDE.md"] becomes moot once AGENTS.md exists.' / AC3: '.codex/config.toml reflects the completed migration'
scenarios: An implementer cannot verify the spike result or find the Codex documentation confirming native AGENTS.md support, leading to uncertainty about whether removing/updating the fallback line is safe.
If Codex's native AGENTS.md support requires a specific Codex version or configuration not present in the repo, the migration could silently degrade agent guidance.
findings.suggested_fix: In the Design decisions section, replace the bare '(spike-validated)' parenthetical with a concrete pointer: e.g., 'Codex reads AGENTS.md natively as primary (validated in spike ticket <ID>; see also https://docs.codex.dev/… or S0 eastbound-bald-raptors).' If `.codex/config.toml` is not yet a repo-tracked file, note that it must be created as part of this story and specify its minimum required content.

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
